heading: Secretary of Energy
label: 60501 Secretary of Energy
text: Except as provided in , the Secretary of Energy has the duties and powers related to the transportation of oil by pipeline that were vested on , in the Interstate Commerce Commission or the chairman or a member of the Commission.section 60502 of this titleOctober 1, 1977
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)6050142:7155., , .Aug. 4, 1977Pub. L. 95–91, § 30691 Stat. 58149:101 (note prec.)., , (2) (related to § 306 of Department of Energy Organization Act), .Oct. 17, 1978Pub. L. 95–473, § 4(c)(1)(A)92 Stat. 1470The words “duties and powers . . . that were vested . . . in” are coextensive with, and substituted for, “transferred . . . such functions set forth in the Interstate Commerce Act and vested by law in” for clarity and to eliminate unnecessary words. The words “on ” are added to reflect the effective date of the transfer of the duties and powers to the Secretary of Energy.October 1, 1977

Abolition of Interstate Commerce Commission and Transfer of FunctionsInterstate Commerce Commission abolished and functions of Commission transferred, except as otherwise provided in , to Surface Transportation Board effective , by , and , set out as a note under . References to Interstate Commerce Commission deemed to refer to Surface Transportation Board, a member or employee of the Board, or Secretary of Transportation, as appropriate, see , set out as a note under .Pub.
